title: The CAUSAL llama decoder block runs FORWARD on the Apple M4 Max GPU — the autoregressive mask on silicon, bit-exact with the recipe
stone: The named next stone from brick 3t / causal-attention-band.fk ('emit lblk-block-causal to Metal — causal jte-llama-block-fwd-msl, the mask is a key-prefix in the threadgroup walk'). The non-causal llama block runs on the GPU (brick 3s, jte-llama-block-fwd-msl) — correct for an encoder, WRONG for autoregressive generation, where a query at position s must NOT see the future. This lowers the proven causal recipe to one GPU kernel: the real generation-ready decoder block on silicon.
device: Apple M4 Max (40-core GPU, unified memory), arm64 Darwin 26.3.1; on-box Metal toolchain (metal/metallib/metal-as), swiftc
algorithm: llama-block.fk lblk-block-causal (fp64) — proven FOUR-WAY 511 by tests/causal-attention-band.fk: identical to lblk-block except each query at position s attends only to keys/values 0..s (the per-query key PREFIX of tb-attn-seq-causal). n1 = RMSNorm1(x) ; h = x + Wo*causal-attn(RoPE(Wq*n1), RoPE(Wk*n1), Wv*n1, scale) ; n2 = RMSNorm2(h) ; y = h + Wdown*SwiGLU(Wgate*n2, Wup*n2) over a SEQUENCE of token vectors. Bias-free llama projections, position = absolute token index. The mask is a loop BOUND, no numeric change to the per-pair dot/softmax — composes the SAME already-four-way recipes as the non-causal block (llama-numerics 4095, rope 4095, transformer-block 511) plus tb-attn-seq-causal/tb-take (transformer-block.fk).
emitter_recipe: form/form-stdlib/jit-tensor-emit.fk — jte-llama-block-fwd-causal-msl (new, next to jte-llama-block-fwd-msl). Byte-identical to the non-causal kernel EXCEPT the attention helper jte-llama-blk-attn-causal: every 'while (t < S)' of the non-causal attention becomes 'while (t < s + 1u)' (5 loops: scores, max, softmax-sum, normalize, value-fold). No other change — same RMSNorm/proj/RoPE/out/FFN folds, same recipe-OWN transcendentals (Newton sqrt, Taylor fexp/fsin/fcos/fln, fpow=fexp(e*fln b)), never Metal's.

emitter_proof_four_way.fourth_arm: 1 band four-way (fkwu + pre-flattened tables), 0 divergent — Go/Rust/TS/fkwu emit byte-identical text; the c0 literal anchor confirms the canonical bytes match the live emitter

on_gpu_execution.carrier: swiftc + Metal.framework host harness — the allowed driver-access carrier per host-kernel.form host-resource-access; runtime makeLibrary, mathMode=safe (IEEE, no fast-math contraction). The kernel LOGIC is Form; only GPU submission rides the host driver.
on_gpu_execution.fixture: the causal-attention-band.fk fixture (identical to llama-block-band.fk): S=2 tokens, d=4, dq=dv=4, HD=4 (single head), FFN hidden 4, scale=1/√4, eps=1e-5, every weight non-trivial
on_gpu_execution.parity_gpu_vs_cpu: max|y_gpu - y_cpu| = 0.0 — BIT-EXACT (same causal folds, both fp32, no contraction)
on_gpu_execution.parity_gpu_vs_fp64_recipe: max|Δ*1e6| = 0.3 — GPU fp32 tracks the proven four-way fp64 recipe within fp32 epsilon

causal_relationships_proven: The GPU output reproduces exactly the values causal-attention-band.fk pins four-way: token 0 (causal) y[0][0]*1e6 = 1893731 (= band c8) — differs from the non-causal block's 1115890 because token 0 no longer sees token 1; the LAST token y[1][0]*1e6 = -1069281 (= band c6) and y[1][2]*1e6 = 1648117 (= band c7) are bit-identical to the non-causal block (its prefix is the whole sequence — the mask is a no-op at the end). The first token attending only to itself is a softmax of a single score = 1.0, no over-masking at the last.
reading: The REAL autoregressive llama decoder block — the one a generation loop steps — now runs forward on the M4 GPU, Form recipe -> Metal -> silicon, bit-for-bit with the four-way-proven causal recipe. The autoregressive mask, which makes a decoder generate one token at a time without leaking the future, is on the hardware. With the load path already Form-native (q4k/q6k/f16 dequant four-way), every llama numeric four-way (llama-numerics, rope), and both the non-causal AND causal block forward now on the GPU, the floor under real local llama inference on the silicon is complete except for: parallelizing across tokens/rows (one threadgroup per query), running over REAL llama3.2:3b GGUF weights on the GPU, and the generation loop (tokenizer + KV cache + greedy decode).
reproduce: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/jit-tensor-emit.fk form-stdlib/tests/llama-block-fwd-causal-emit-band.fk && cd .. && bash scripts/metal_llama_block_causal_audit.sh
